steps.title: What Are Conic Sections?
steps.introText: Conic sections are shapes created when a flat plane cuts through a double cone. By changing the angle of the plane, we can create circles, ellipses, parabolas, and hyperbolas.
steps.prompt: Explore the cone and its parts, then continue when you're ready.
Move the plane around and see what happens to the cross section.
hints: What happens if the plane cuts both cones?

feedback.correct: Great! A circle is formed when the plane cuts straight across one cone.
feedback.incorrect: This slice isn't a circle yet.
feedback.hint: Try making the plane more horizontal.

feedback.correct: Nice work! An ellipse forms when the plane is tilted but still cuts only one cone.
feedback.incorrect: You're close, but this slice is producing a different conic.
feedback.hint: Tilt the plane slightly without making it parallel to the side of the cone.

steps.miniReflection: Notice how the circle stretched into an oval shape.

feedback.correct: Excellent! A parabola appears when the plane is parallel to the cone's side.
feedback.hint: Match the slope of the cone's edge.

feedback.correct: Perfect! Because the plane cuts both cones, the cross section becomes a hyperbola.
feedback.hint: Can you make the plane pass through both cones?

steps.prompt: What determines which conic section appears?
steps.feedback: Exactly. The angle of the plane relative to the cone determines the shape.

steps.completionMessage: You've discovered all four conic sections! In the next lesson, we'll focus on parabolas and see how they appear everywhere from satellite dishes to car headlights.
